In the previous section, we learned how React provides the Event Object and how event.target.value helps us read user input.
Now let’s build something every real-world application needs.
A Form.
Whether you’re logging into Gmail, registering on LinkedIn, placing an order on Amazon, or filling out a contact form, every application relies on forms.
React provides a clean way to handle them.
Let’s understand how.
Understanding Form Submission
Imagine you’re filling out a login form.
You enter:

• Email

• Password
Finally, you click Login.
In a traditional HTML application, something immediately happens.
The page refreshes.
Why?
Because that’s the browser’s default behavior.
HTML forms automatically submit data and reload the page.
React applications work differently.
